title: AT&T (T)Intrinsic Value
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on AT&T using actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what AT&T is really worth.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
">
Telecom is a highly saturated, capital-intensive industry. We don't expect rapid expansion, but AT&T can maintain slow growth through 5G and fiber upgrades, alongside disciplined cost controls.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
">
Telecom is a highly saturated, capital-intensive industry. We don't expect rapid expansion, but AT&T can maintain slow growth through 5G and fiber upgrades, alongside disciplined cost controls.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
">
Telecom is a highly saturated, capital-intensive industry. We don't expect rapid expansion, but AT&T can maintain slow growth through 5G and fiber upgrades, alongside disciplined cost controls.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use DCF instead of a P/E or EV/EBITDA multiple?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "AT&T (T) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.
